seoTitle: How to Merge Tickets in Freshdesk
metaDescription: Learn how to merge duplicate or related tickets in Freshdesk to keep your queue clean and conversation history in one place.
intro: When a customer submits multiple tickets about the same issue, merging them keeps your team from duplicating effort. This guide shows how to combine related tickets into one in Freshdesk.

steps.heading: Open the primary ticket
steps.body: Decide which ticket will remain as the main one and open it in your Freshdesk dashboard.

steps.heading: Select the merge option
steps.body: Use the merge action, typically found in the ticket's menu of actions.

steps.heading: Choose the tickets to merge
steps.body: Search for and select the duplicate or related tickets you want to combine into the primary ticket.

steps.heading: Confirm the merge
steps.body: Review the selection and confirm, which combines the conversation history into the single primary ticket.

faq.question: What happens to the merged ticket's history?
faq.answer: The conversation history from merged tickets is typically added to the primary ticket so context isn't lost.

faq.question: Can I merge tickets from different customers?
faq.answer: Merging is generally intended for tickets from the same customer or about the same issue, not unrelated requests.

faq.question: Can I undo a merge?
faq.answer: Once tickets are merged, undoing it isn't always straightforward, so it's worth double-checking before confirming.

faq.question: Does merging close the duplicate tickets?
faq.answer: Yes, the duplicate tickets are typically closed or archived once merged into the primary ticket.
